task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Field_Survey' track is visible in a GIS/GPS application map view or attribute table.

task_completion.description: Agent locates the high-speed driving segment at the end of the track.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The agent selects or highlights the points at the end of the track, which typically have larger spatial gaps between them indicating higher speeds.

task_completion.description: Agent ensures the dense walking survey points are kept intact.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The dense cluster of points representing the foot survey remains visible, unselected, and undeleted on the map.

task_completion.description: Agent removes the high-speed driving segment.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The selected high-speed points at the end of the track are deleted and disappear from the map or list of track points.

task_completion.description: Agent exports the cleaned track to the specified path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A save or export dialog is visible showing the file being saved as a GPX format to the exact path 'C:\workspace\output\cleaned_survey.gpx'.

integrity.description: Agent used a GIS/GPS graphical interface to edit the track.
integrity.visual_evidence: The track editing and point deletion were performed within a GIS application (like QGIS) rather than by writing a script.

integrity.description: Agent used the software's export/save feature to create the final GPX file.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent interacts with the 'Save As' or 'Export' menus in the application rather than using terminal commands to mock or copy the output.

integrity.description: Agent did not manually edit the GPX XML structure in a text editor.
integrity.visual_evidence: There should be no screenshots of the agent opening a GPX file in Notepad or another text editor to manually delete XML nodes.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Verified facts: The expected output path is exactly C:\workspace\output\cleaned_survey.gpx. The cleaned track must have a maximum allowed speed of less than 10.0 m/s and a minimum retention ratio of 0.90. Note: Due to a structural bug in the task setup, the 'Field_Survey' track is never injected into the environment, meaning the agent will likely fail to find the source file to complete the task.
